chapter_title.en: Chapter of Beginning at Mecca and Ending at Medina

text: 3141 - صَفْوَانَ عَنِ اَلْعِيصِ بْنِ اَلْقَاسِمِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلْحُجَّاجِ مِنَ اَلْكُوفَةِ يَبْدَءُونَ بِالْمَدِينَةِ أَفْضَلُ أَوْ بِمَكَّةَ فَقَالَ « بِالْمَدِينَةِ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3141 - Safwan narrated from Al-'Iys ibn Al-Qasim, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the pilgrims from Kufa—whether starting with Medina or Mecca is better.
Imam (as) replied: "With Medina."
